content:
# Final Review Proposal
**Producer:** agent:codex/gpt-5.4-xhigh

## Summary

Review found three fix-now issues: mixed short/qualified bead refs are still matched exactly in retry/finalization write paths, snapshot reconciliation still double-counts mixed-form refs as separate beads, and task detail queries now regress to hard errors when milestone metadata is missing.

## Amendments

1. finding_summary: Mixed short/qualified bead IDs are only normalized on the read side. `record_task_run_start` can discover a prior finalized attempt via canonicalized matching, but `reopen_failed_exact_attempt` still filters raw rows with `entry.bead_id == bead_id` at src/adapters/fs.rs:4541-4550, and `finalize_task_run_internal` does the same at src/adapters/fs.rs:4693-4699. If lineage stored `bead-1` and the current task uses `ms-alpha.bead-1` (or vice versa), retries and completion/repair syncs fail with "already finalized" or "no matching task run" even though the attempt was found. severity: high. affected_files: [src/adapters/fs.rs]. remediation_hint: Canonicalize bead refs in the raw-row reopen/finalize paths too, and add retry/complete tests that mix short and qualified bead IDs.
   Rationale: This patch explicitly adds mixed-form bead-id handling for history/query paths, so leaving the write paths on exact string comparison produces real execution failures on the same legacy data shape.
2. finding_summary: Snapshot reconciliation still treats short and qualified bead refs as different beads. `latest_task_runs_per_bead` keys by raw `entry.bead_id` at src/contexts/milestone_record/model.rs:554-568, and `reconcile_snapshot_from_lineage` uses that output for `active_bead` and progress counts at src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:953-989. A retry history containing both `bead-1` and `ms-alpha.bead-1` for one logical bead will be double-counted, and two active mixed-form rows can even trip the "multiple active beads present in lineage" corruption path. severity: medium. affected_files: [src/contexts/milestone_record/model.rs, src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs]. remediation_hint: Group latest runs by canonical milestone bead ref rather than the raw stored string.
   Rationale: The new change acknowledges mixed-form bead IDs, so reconciliation has to collapse them consistently or the milestone snapshot diverges from the query/view of the same lineage.
3. finding_summary: `project show` / `task show` now fail outright when a bead-linked project's milestone record is missing. `show_project` unconditionally loads lineage at src/contexts/project_run_record/service.rs:1363-1417, and `read_bead_lineage` returns `MilestoneNotFound` as soon as the milestone directory is absent at src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:2222-2237. That turns an otherwise readable project into an error even though the project record, run snapshot, and raw milestone/bead IDs still exist. severity: medium. affected_files: [src/contexts/project_run_record/service.rs, src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs]. remediation_hint: Degrade to `task_lineage: None` or a partial lineage stub when the milestone record is missing instead of aborting the whole detail query.
   Rationale: The lineage enrichment is additive context; it should not make existing task detail queries unusable when external milestone metadata has been removed or has not been materialized yet.
